original: AmericanSimone Bileswins all-round gold at the World Championships in Antwerp, Belgium, - becoming the most decorated gymnast ever with 34 world and Olympic medals
Jason Lewis completes the first human-powered circumnavigation of the globe.
New York Yankees knuckleballerPhil Niekrobecomes 18th pitcher to win 300 games; at 46 becomes oldest to pitch a shut-out, beating Toronto 8-0
New research confirms fossil footprints 23,000-21,000 years old at White Sands National Park, New Mexico, probably the oldest evidence of humans in the Americas
Tom Jager, American swimmer (5 Olympic gold 1984, 88, 92; 50m freestyle world record x 6), born in East St. Louis, Illinois
PaleoanthropologistMary Leakeyfinds the first partial fossil skull of Proconsul africanus, an ancestor of apes and humans on Rusinga Island, Kenya
Phillies ace Roy Halladay pitches only the 2nd postseason no-hitter in MLB history in Game 1 NLDS, 4-0 v Cincinnati Reds, at Citizens Bank Park, Philadelphia
Liu Yang, Chinese taikonaut, 1st Chinese woman in space aboard  Shenzhou 9, born in Zhengzhou, Henan province, China
After 9 seasons & 3 Championships with the Chicago Bulls,Michael Jordanannounces his retirement from the NBA; returns on March 18, 1995 and leads Bulls to another 3 NBA titles
WHO Director-GeneralTedros Adhanom Ghebreyesusrecommends world's first Malaria vaccine (Mosquirix) for children after a pilot program was effective in Africa
Svend Karlsen, Norwegian strongman and powerlifter, 2001 World's Strongest Man, born in Drammen, Norway
Tavern owner "Billy Goat" Sianis buys seat for his goat for Game 4 of Baseball World Series, is escorted out and casts goat curse on Chicago Cubs
ScientistAlbert Sabinannounces that his oral polio vaccine is ready for testing; it would soon supplant Jonas Salk's vaccine in many parts of the world
Nobel Prize for Physics awarded to Roger Penrose, Reinhard Genzel and Andrea M. Ghez for work on black holes
US 67th manned space mission STS 41 (Discovery 11) launches into orbit
First known ascent of the summit of Mt Kilimanjaro, Africa's highest peak, by German climber Hans Meyer and Austrian Ludwig Purtscheller
US nuclear sub USS Seawolf remains a record 60 days under the north pole
51 Pegasi discovered as the first major star, apart from the Sun, to have a planet orbiting around it
Martin Behaim, German navigator and geographer, designed the Erdapfel, the oldest terrestrial globe in the world, born in Nuremberg, Germany (d. 1507)

New Zealand wins inaugural Rugby Championship with a 32-16 win over South Africa in Johannesburg; undefeated All Blacks score 4 tries to 1 withDan Carterbooting 3 conversions, a penalty & dropped goal
Australia records the biggest comeback in Rugby Championship history in beating Argentina 45-34 in Salta; Wallabies trial 31-7 at halftime but score 5 second half tries to none to overwhelm Pumas
Kevin SystromandMike Kriegerlaunch social media app Instagram
Jazz Jennings, American transgender activist, born in South Florida (probable date)
Ryan Kaji, American Youtuber (Ryan's World), born in California
Lois & Clark (fictional characters) wed
Beatrix van Rijk becomes 1st licensed Dutch woman pilot
Solar Polar Orbiter 'Ulysses' launched
Matt Vogel, American puppeteer (Sesame Street, 1996-present; Kermit the Frog, 2017-present; Big Bird, 2018-present), born in Kansas City, Kansas
Rebecca Lobo, American WNBA basketball center (Olympic gold, 1996; NY Liberty), born in Hartford, Connecticut
Vegas Golden Knights make their NHL debut in Dallas, winning 2-1 over the Stars
New Zealand claims 6th overall, and 3rd straight Rugby Championship with 32-20 win over South Africa in Pretoria; All Blacks outscore Springboks 4 tries to 3
Jeff B. Davis, American comedian and impressionist known for " Whose Line Is It Anyway?", born in Los Angeles, California
Riccardo Giacconi, Italian American astrophysicist, Nobel Prize for Physics 2002 for X-ray astronomy, born in Genoa, Italy (d. 2018)
Loyle Carner [Benjamin Coyle-Larner], English rapper (Not Waving, but Drowning), born in London
Benjamin Hanks patents self-winding clock
Agatha Christie's play "The Mousetrap" opens in London (still running)
MESSENGER spacecraft performs a second Mercury flyby
Nobel Prize for Chemistry awarded to Benjamin List and David W.C. MacMillan for development of asymmetric organocatalysis (tool for building molecules)
French Revolution:Louis XVIreturns to Paris from Versailles after being confronted by the Parisian women on 5 October
NHL New York IslandersMike Bossy's 25th career hat trick
French McLaren driverAlain Prostclinches his first Formula 1 World Drivers Championship with a 4th placing in the European Grand Prix at Brands Hatch
The Beatles promotional films (music videos) for "Hey Jude" and "Revolution" first broadcast in the US on "The Smothers Brothers Comedy Hour"
Nobel prize for Physics awarded to Takaaki Kajita (Japan) and Arthur McDonald (Canada) for work on neutrinos
Fisk Jubilee Singers, an African-American a cappella ensembe from Nashville's Fisk University, begin 1st national tour of US in Cincinnati, Ohio
Zoological Gardens opens at Sloat & Skyline Boulevards in San Francisco, California
Canadians free Austria
Ernest Walton, Irish physicist (Nobel Prize 1951 for work building earliest particle accelerator), born in Abbeyside, Ireland (d. 1995)
Meret Oppenheim, German born Swiss surrealist artist and photographer (Object: Le Déjeuner en fourrure), born in Berlin, Germany (d. 1985)
Millie Small, Jamaican ska singer, and songwriter ("My Boy Lollipop"), born in Clarendon, Jamaica (d. 2020)
At the Hollywood Bowl, actor John Cusack strolls on stage and hands singerPeter Gabriela boombox prior to performance of the song "In Your Eyes" in tribute to the scene in the film "Say Anything..."
Kathrin Dörre-Heinig, German athlete (3 x London Marathon, 3 x Tokyo Marathon, 3 x Hamburg Marathon; Olympic bronze 1988), born in Leipzig, Germany
Soviet Luna 3, 1st successful photographic spacecraft, impacts Moon
Hannah H Gray inaugurated as 1st female head of a US university (Chicago)
Colorodo Avalanche (former Que Nordiques) 1st NHL game, beat Detroit
Carnegie Hall in NYC re-opens after 18 month pandemic shutdown with a concert by The Philadelphia Orchestra, conducted by Yannick Nézet-Séguin
The Beatles release the single "Something" in US; it is the first "A" side written byGeorge Harrison
Tony Dungy, American Pro Football HOF coach (Super Bowl XLI Indianapolis Colts; TB Buccaneers; Maxwell Club NFL Coach of the Year 1997, 2005), born in Jackson, Michigan
Barbie Hsu, Taiwanese actress (Meteor Garden), Mando-pop singer, and television personality, born in Taipei City, Taiwan (d. 2025)[1]
Jamie Laurie, American rapper-rocker (Flobots - "Handlebars"), born in Denver, Colorado
1st World Series Baseball Game 1: Cincinnati Red Stockings (AA) beat Chicago White Stockings (NL), 4-0 at Bank Street Grounds, Cincinnati
